statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 20 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, while crude oil prices have surged 45% since late February.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as widespread stock depletion follows recent strategic reserve releases to address Middle East supply disruptions. Of the 27 EU countries, 20 hold critical reserves in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, Luxembourg, and Germany showing critical levels across all three fuel types. Despite EU-wide averages appearing adequate at 98.9 days for petrol, 97.8 days for diesel, and 91.9 days for jet fuel, the distribution is heavily skewed by a handful of well-stocked nations.

Jet fuel presents the most acute vulnerability, with 19 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold, including severely depleted reserves in Poland (21.3 days) and Portugal (23.1 days). Major economies are particularly exposed, with Germany holding just 61.4 days of jet fuel reserves and France at 59.6 days. Diesel shortages affect 14 countries, while petrol reserves are critical in 12 nations.

The current situation reflects the compound effect of strategic reserve releases and a 45.3% surge in Brent crude prices since late February, from $72.48 to $105.33 per barrel. This price escalation has driven retail fuel costs to an EU average of €1.764 per liter for petrol and €1.943 for diesel, with significant variation across member states. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol prices at €2.279 per liter, while Malta enjoys the lowest at €1.34 per liter.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries hold critical reserves in at least one fuel type, indicating widespread depletion following strategic reserve releases
Jet fuel shows the greatest vulnerability with 19 of 27 countries below the 90-day minimum, including Poland at just 21.3 days
Major economies Germany, France, and Italy all hold critical reserves across multiple fuel categories
Brent crude oil has surged 45.3% since late February to $105.33/barrel, driving up retail fuel prices across the EU
Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types at critical levels: petrol 33.2 days, diesel 32.4 days, jet fuel 76.4 days
